summary: Transcript eval uses 0.6 threshold (vs 0.8 for synthetic) with aggregate average assertions instead of per-scorer
context: Real session transcripts have higher variance than synthetic scenarios — individual scorer results fluctuate because real usage patterns don't follow ideal workflows. Needed a meaningful quality signal without excessive false failures.
rationale: Lower threshold (0.6) accounts for real-world messiness while still differentiating good vs poor sessions. Aggregate average assertions (rather than per-scorer) prevent flaky tests from natural variance in individual dimensions. Phase 19 will tune thresholds with more data.

alternatives.reason_rejected: Real transcripts consistently score lower due to natural usage patterns — would cause constant failures without actionable signal
Individual scorer variance on real data too high — would create flaky tests that don't indicate real quality issues
